what_it_does: Defines an SVG open-lock icon using stroked vector geometry in a 24 by 24 viewBox.
observations: Uses an inline SVG root with xmlns and viewBox="0 0 24 24".
Sets stroke-width="1.5" with round line caps and joins.
Draws the lock body as a rounded rectangle at x="5", y="11", width="14", height="9", rx="1.5".
Draws the open shackle with a single path from the body upward and across.
conventions: Follows common line-icon SVG conventions: no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded caps, rounded joins.
Keeps icon geometry in basic rect and path elements.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present for standalone accessibility semantics.
notes: A minimal reusable open-lock SVG icon intended to inherit color from its rendering context.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename implies an open-lock icon, and the SVG content draws an open-lock icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a simple unlocked-state icon that can adapt to surrounding text or UI color.
contribution: Contributes a reusable open-lock vector asset with currentColor styling, rounded stroke treatment, and a compact 24x24 line-icon form.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG line icon depicting an open lock with a rounded rectangular body and open shackle path.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing an unlocked or open-access state as a simple themeable interface icon.
